title: TDF-Tracker
description: Interactive dashboard tracking rider performance, stage results, and fantasy points for the 2026 Tour de France. Six views spanning jerseys, stage-by-stage rider breakdowns, team aggregates, and a browser-local fantasy team builder.

detail.tagline: A live dashboard for the 2026 Tour de France — real race results and official Tissot fantasy scoring in one place.
detail.role: Solo — scrapers, data pipeline, and dashboard.
overview: The Tour de France runs 21 stages over three weeks, and following both the real race and a Tissot fantasy league means juggling a dozen tabs. TDF-Tracker pulls them together: live stage results, jersey holders, and every rider's official fantasy points across six connected views, refreshed as each stage settles.

The fantasy numbers are the real thing — pulled straight from the Tissot game's authenticated API so captain doublings, jersey bonuses, and the daily bonus question all reconcile exactly, rather than being re-derived from the rulebook. An early attempt to recompute points drifted on special stages (the opening team time trial scores each rider as a fraction of the team's result), so the tracker trusts the source of truth instead.

gallery.caption: Overview — race state at a glance: stage progress, jersey holders, my league rank, and top fantasy scorers broken down by points source.
Stages — the real letour.fr result for each stage (finish order, times) paired with my fantasy scorecard for that day.
Riders — every rider's official Tissot points with a click-through breakdown by source: stage finishes, sprints, climbs, and jerseys.
Value — a cost-vs-points scatter and an optimizer that builds the best legal 8-rider squad within the 120-star budget.
My Team — my squad's per-stage points, cumulative total and rank, and the live league standings.

features.title: Six connected views
features.detail: Overview KPIs and jerseys, per-stage results, every rider's points, team aggregates, a value optimizer, and a personal My-Team dashboard.

features.title: Official Tissot scoring
features.detail: Fantasy points come straight from the game's API, so every captain bonus, jersey award, and bonus-question point matches the official standings.

features.title: Real race + fantasy, joined
features.detail: Each stage pairs the actual letour.fr result — finish order, GC, jerseys, time gaps — with the fantasy scorecard for that day.

features.title: Value optimizer
features.detail: A cost-vs-points scatter and a solver that assembles the best legal 8-rider squad within the 120-star budget.

features.title: Personal league tracking
features.detail: My-Team view shows your squad's per-stage points, cumulative rank, and live league standings.

challenges.title: Trust the game, don't re-derive it
challenges.problem: Fantasy points look computable from the rules, but special stages break naive scoring — the opening team time trial, for instance, scores each rider as a fraction of the team's result.
challenges.outcome: Rather than recompute, the tracker pulls official Tissot scores directly from the game's authenticated API, so captain doublings, jersey bonuses, and the bonus question reconcile with the real standings exactly.

challenges.title: Two data sources, one clean model
challenges.problem: The official points API omits rider costs and knows nothing about the actual race, while letour.fr has results but no fantasy data — and rider names differ across both.
challenges.outcome: A Python build step joins the authenticated API to a rider-cost table via an accent- and ligature-folded name key, layers in scraped letour.fr race results, and emits one JSON the app loads.

challenges.title: Per-stage points without a per-stage feed
challenges.problem: The API returns cumulative season totals, not what each rider scored on a given stage — but the whole dashboard hinges on per-stage numbers.
challenges.outcome: The pipeline snapshots the cumulative totals after every stage and differences consecutive snapshots to recover per-stage points for all riders, while the personal squad gets exact per-stage scores from the game's match-sheet endpoint.

stack.name: Recharts
stack.why: Stacked points-by-source bars and the cost-vs-points scatter.

stack.name: Python
stack.why: Scrapes both feeds, joins them, and builds the single data file.

stack.name: Tissot API + letour.fr
stack.why: Official fantasy scores plus real race results.
